Build better
    decks faster
    Vibe-Coding Presentations with Claude Code
    Step 1
    The Origin Story
    How it started

    Found an article about using AI to "vibe-code" slides as a web-based presentation instead of relying on a traditional PowerPoint workflow. That led me to the Frontend Slides Claude Code plugin, which helps generate polished HTML/CSS/JS slide decks with animations, bold visual styles, and a "show, don't tell" design process.

    Instead of asking for vague design direction, the plugin walks through key questions like: What is the presentation for? How long should it be? What content already exists? What style direction do you want? What kind of audience is it for? It then creates a draft deck for you to review and refine.

    The article
    I Vibe-Coded My Slides Instead of PowerPoint

    xda-developers.com ↗

    Plugin
    frontend-slides

    github.com/zarazhangrui/frontend-slides ↗

    Step 2
    What I Handed Claude
    Input 1
    Google Doc

    Details I wanted on each slide — copy, structure, story flow.

    Input 2
    Run /frontend-slides

    The plugin asks about your goals, content, and style then drafts the full deck for you to review.

    Result
    9-slide draft in ~10 min

    Structured, scrollable, fully animated — a real starting point, not a blank page.

    Step 3
    The restyle that mattered

    First pass was fine. Not on brand. So I dropped in these four Salesforce assets and Claude reskinned every slide to match.

    1
    Salesforce Color Palette
    The official brand colors — Cloud Blue, Electric Blue, and their tints
    2
    Typography
    Avant Garde for Salesforce Demi for headlines · Salesforce Sans for body copy
    3
    Salesforce Logo
    A downloadable version of the logo
    4
    Astro Animations
    GIF versions of Astro characters with transparent backgrounds
    Brand assets — Color
    Salesforce Color Palette
    Salesforce Presentation Library ↗
    Brand assets — Typography
    Words Matter
    Salesforce Brand Typography ↗

    Avant Garde — A bespoke adaptation of the 1970 geometric classic. Use the Demi weight with short, 3–6 word hero copy for main headlines. Also use it for subheads and CTAs that need to stand out.

    Salesforce Sans — Use Salesforce Sans Regular for all scenarios beyond headlines.

    Brand assets — Logo
    Salesforce Logo
    Salesforce Asset Library ↗
    Placement
    Positioned top-right on every slide using position:absolute with a transparent background so it sits cleanly over any slide color.
    Claude resized it to clamp(36px, 4.5vw, 56px) — small enough to stay out of the way, large enough to be recognizable at any screen size.
    Step 4
    The cool bit: real animations
    Astro & Agent Astro Portal ↗

    The Astro files from the Asset Portal are high-quality videos with transparent backgrounds. Drop one in and Claude handles the rest — converting it into formats that play in every browser, looping silently, with no background box.

    What Claude does
    • Takes the original Astro video file
    • Converts it to two browser-friendly formats — one for Chrome & Firefox, one for Safari
    • Keeps the transparent background in both
    • Wires it up to autoplay, loop, and run silently on your slide
    The prompt to steal
    Paste this into Claude Code
    I have an Astro video file at [file path]. Please:
    1. Convert it to VP9 WebM for Chrome/Firefox and HEVC MOV for Safari, preserving the transparent background
    2. Embed both versions as base64 directly in my HTML slide
    3. Place it at the [bottom-right / bottom-left] of slide [#], looping automatically with no sound

    Replace the highlighted fields with your file path, position, and slide number. Claude handles the encoding and wiring automatically.

    Step 5
    Make it
    reusable

    Asked Claude to save this information in its memory whenever it uses the frontend-slides plugin — so every future deck is Salesforce-branded by default.

    Saved to memory
    Color palette, typography rules, logo placement, Astro animation commands
    Next time
    Run /frontend-slides and Claude already knows the brand — no re-explaining needed
    Now you try
    Download the plugin → Run the command → Add your visuals → Vibe-code the deck
